Bad Debt Expense

Estela Bobadilla-Cruz

2023-01-05

Introduction

This analysis of HDC’s 2021 financial statement and bad debt expense data explores if there is a correlation between bad debt and rental income, vacancy, and or a building’s financial health. A building’s financial health is measured by the debt service coverage ratio (DSCR), defined as net operating income divided by debt service. Generally, we prefer that buildings have a DSCR > 1.15 or DSCR > 1.2; this tells us they can pay its full mortgage bill.A DSCR < 1 raises concern, as it likely indicates a building will not be able to pay it full mortgage bill. In total, we utilize 373 active projects that submitted data in 2021.

Analysis Agenda


Part I displays a Histogram of bad debt expense per unit.

Part II displays Boxplots of Bad Debt Expense/Unit by Project Based Rental Assistance Status, Program Type, and Occupancy Type

Part III displays Scatterplots of Effective Gross Rent/Unit, Effective Residential Gross Rent/Unit, Total Vacancies/Unit, DSCR1, DSCR2 versus Bad Debt Expense



Part One: Histogram of Bad Debt Expense/Unit

## [1] "The Median of Bad Debt Expense/Unit is $434.54"

#----Section: Scatter plots of bde values and dscr1
#
#Scatterplot of DSCR1 (note: this is NOI / annual mortgage payments for all mortgages) vs. bad debt expense per unit.
dscr_bde <- rankCols %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = bdeRank, y = dscrRank)) + 
  #fill = "dodgerblue3"
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values=c('light blue','dark Gray')) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),lim = c(0, 100)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),lim = c(0,  100) ) +

  #geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) + #linear regression for best fit line
  labs(title = "BDE Percentile Rank vs. DSCR1 Percentile Rank ", x = "BDE/Unit", y = "DSCR1/Unit") +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(legend.position="none",axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 15, b = 0, l=0))) 

#Display Graph
#dscr_bde
#lm()
#-4/4

Part Two: Boxplot of Bad Debt Expense/Unit by Project Based Rental Assistance Status, Program Type, and Occupancy Type

#----Section: Task Outputs----
#5. Boxplot of bad debt expense per unit by PBRA (project based rental assistance) status. (We expect bad debt to be lower for projects with PBRA.)



bde_by_PBRA <- joinedData %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = bad_debt_ex_byUnit, x = as.factor(pbra), fill = pbra)) + 
  #fill = "dodgerblue3"
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(startpoint, endpoint, intervals),lim = c(startpoint-50, endpoint), labels=scales::dollar_format()) + 
  labs(title = "Bad Debt Expense/Unit by Project Based Rental Assistance", x = "Project Based Rental Assistance Status", y = "Bad Debt Expense/Unit", caption = paste("Excludes ",nrow(filter(joinedData, bad_debt_ex_byUnit > endpoint))," HDC Projects with bde > $", round(endpoint, 2), sep = "")) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 15, b = 0, l=0))) 

#Rename Title PBRA / elaborate 
bde_by_PBRA

# plot info 
# ddply(joinedData,summarise,median=median(bad_debt_ex_by_Unit))
#----Section: Task Outputs----

#3. Boxplot of bad debt expense per unit by program type.


#Checking Programs with only 1 hdc project
#Servicing Only 
#servOnly = filter(joinedData, program_type == "Servicing Only")
#servOnly$bad_debt_ex #215.64
#city8A = filter(joinedData, program_type == "City 8A")
#city8A$bad_debt_ex #213.07
#joinedData = filter(joinedData, program_type != "Servicing Only")
#joinedData = filter(joinedData, program_type != "City 8A")
#sort by median 

#reorder before
other_programs$new_program_type = with(other_programs, reorder(new_program_type , bad_debt_ex_byUnit, median , na.rm=TRUE))
#with(other_programs, reorder(new_program_type , bad_debt_ex_byUnit, median , na.rm=TRUE))
#reorder(other_programs$new_program_type , other_programs$bad_debt_ex_byUnit, median , na.rm=TRUE)
#aes( x = reorder(new_program_type, bad_debt_ex_byUnit, median, na.rm = TRUE),                    
#with negative bde 
all_bde_byProgram <- other_programs %>%
  #ggplot(all_bde_byProgram%>% arrange(region, desc(value)) )
  # aes( x = reorder(new_program_type, bad_debt_ex_byUnit, median, na.rm = TRUE),
  ggplot( aes( x =  bad_debt_ex_byUnit, y = as.factor(new_program_type), fill = new_program_type) ) + 
  #fill = "dodgerblue3"
  geom_boxplot( alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(startpoint-100, endpoint, intervals),lim = c(startpoint-100, endpoint), labels=scales::dollar_format()) +
  labs(title = "Bad Debt Expense/Unit by Program Type", y = "Program Type", x = "Bad Debt Expense/Unit", caption = paste("Excludes program types with < 10 projects and ",nrow(filter(joinedData, bad_debt_ex_byUnit > endpoint))," projects with bde > $", round(endpoint, 2), sep = "")) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(legend.position="none", axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 15, b = 0, l=0))) 

all_bde_byProgram

#----Section: Task Outputs----
#4. Boxplot of bad debt expense per unit by occupancy type

#reorder before
#joinedData$occupancy_type = with(joinedData, reorder(occupancy_type , bad_debt_ex_byUnit, median, na.rm=TRUE))

bde_byOcc <- joinedData %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = bad_debt_ex_byUnit, x = as.factor(occupancy_type), fill = occupancy_type)) + 
  #fill = "dodgerblue3"
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(-200, 1400, 200),lim = c(-200, 1400), labels=scales::dollar_format()) + 
  labs(title = "Bad Debt Expense/Unit by Occupancy Type", x = "Occupancy Type", y = "Bad Debt Expense/Unit", caption = paste("Excludes ",nrow(filter(joinedData, bad_debt_ex_byUnit > endpoint))," HDC projects with bde > $", round(endpoint, 2), sep = "")) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(legend.position="none",axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 15, b = 0, l=0))) 

bde_byOcc

#----Section: Task Outputs----
#5. Boxplot of bad debt expense per unit by PBRA (project based rental assistance) status. (We expect bad debt to be lower for projects with PBRA.)


bde_by_PBRA <- joinedData %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = bad_debt_ex_byUnit, x = as.factor(pbra), fill = pbra)) + 
  #fill = "dodgerblue3"
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(-200, 1400, 200),lim = c(-200, 1400), labels=scales::dollar_format()) + 
  labs(title = "Bad Debt Expense/Unit by Project Based Rental Assistance (PBRA)", x = "PBRA", y = "Bad Debt Expense/Unit", caption = paste("Excludes ",nrow(filter(joinedData, bad_debt_ex_byUnit > endpoint))," HDC projects with bde > $", round(endpoint, 2), sep = "")) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(legend.position="none",axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 15, b = 0, l=0)))

Part Three: Scatterplot of Effective Gross Rent/Unit, Effective Residential Gross Rent/Unit, Total Vacancies/Unit, DSCR1, DSCR2 versus Bad Debt Expense

#----Section: Task Outputs----

#6. Scatterplot of effective gross rent per unit (x) vs. bad debt expense per unit (y). (For all scatterplots, set bad debt expense to the y axis. Our outcome variable of interest typically goes on the y axis.)
#shape = program_type, color = program_type
#shape = pbra, color = pbra
#Info for graphing purposes
grossrent_quant = array(quantile(joinedData$EFF_GROSSRENT_byUnit, probs = c(0, .05, .95,1)))
gr_minval = grossrent_quant[1]
gr_percent5 = grossrent_quant[2]
gr_percent95 = grossrent_quant[3]
gr_maxval = grossrent_quant[4]
gr_startpoint = round(gr_minval, 0)
gr_endpoint = gr_maxval 

#95 percentile val, only 5% is higher, so 
#if max is 2x larger than 95 percentile, we will set our end point 
if(gr_maxval >= (2*gr_percent95)){
  gr_endpoint = gr_percent95}
if(abs(gr_minval) >= (2*gr_percent5)){
  gr_startpoint = round(gr_percent5, 0)
}


gr_intervals = round(gr_endpoint/5,0)
if(gr_startpoint<0){
  gr_intervals = abs(gr_startpoint)
}else{
  #greater that zero
  gr_startpoint = 0
}




egr_bde <- joinedData %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = EFF_GROSSRENT_byUnit, y = bad_debt_ex_byUnit)) + 
  #, fill = pbra
  #fill = "dodgerblue3"
  geom_point() +
 # scale_color_manual(values=c('light blue','dark Gray')) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1500, 500),lim = c(0, 1500), labels=scales::dollar_format()) +
 scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, gr_endpoint, gr_intervals),lim = c(0, gr_endpoint), labels=scales::dollar_format()) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE,colour="black") + #linear regression for best fit line
  labs(title = "Effective Gross Rent vs. Bad Debt Expense", x = "Effective Gross Rent/Unit", y = "Bad Debt Expense/Unit", caption = paste("Excludes ", nrow(filter(joinedData, EFF_GROSSRENT_byUnit > gr_endpoint))," HDC projects with Effective Gross Rent > $", round(gr_endpoint, 2), sep = "")) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(legend.position="none",axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 15, b = 0, l=0))) 

#Display Graph
egr_bde

#lm(bad_debt_ex ~ EFF_GROSSRENT, joinedData)
#get rid out of outlier 
#note of the impact of rem outliers
#redefine prog type as 'other' for progs w. < 10 proj
#----Section: Task Outputs----

#7. Scatterplot of effective residential gross rent per unit vs. bad debt expense per unit. (We want to see if the story changes when we ignore nonresidential rent.)


resGR_bde <- joinedData %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = EFF_RES_GRENT_byUnit, y = bad_debt_ex_byUnit)) + 
  #, color = pbra
  #fill = "dodgerblue3"
  geom_point() +
  #scale_color_manual(values=c('light blue','dark Gray')) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1500, 300),lim = c(0, 1500), labels=scales::dollar_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 50000, 10000),lim = c(0,  50000), labels=scales::dollar_format()) +


  
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE,colour="black") + #linear regression for best fit line
  labs(title = "Effective Residential Gross Rent vs. Bad Debt Expense Per Unit", x = "Effective Residential Gross Rent/Unit", y = "Bad Debt Expense/Unit",caption = paste("Excludes ",nrow(filter(joinedData, EFF_RES_GRENT_byUnit > 50000))," HDC projects with Effective Residential Gross Rent/Unit > $", round(50000, 2), sep = "")) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(legend.position="none", axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 15, b = 0, l=0))) 

#Display Graph
resGR_bde

#lm()
#----Section: Task Outputs----

#8. Scatterplot of total vacancies per unit vs. bad debt expense per unit.

#joinedData$TOTAL_VACANCIES_byUnit[joinedData$TOTAL_VACANCIES_byUnit == NULL] <- 0
joinedData$TOTAL_VACANCIES_byUnit[joinedData$TOTAL_VACANCIES_byUnit == 0] <- NA
joinedData$TOTAL_VACANCIES_byUnit = abs(joinedData$TOTAL_VACANCIES_byUnit)


vac_bde <- joinedData %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = TOTAL_VACANCIES_byUnit, y = bad_debt_ex_byUnit)) + 
  #fill = "dodgerblue3"
  geom_point() +
  #scale_color_manual(values=c('light blue','dark Gray')) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200),lim = c(0, 1000), labels=scales::dollar_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200),lim = c(0,  1000)) +



 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE,colour="black") + #linear regression for best fit line
  labs(title = "Total Vacancies vs. Bad Debt Expense Per Unit", x = "Total Vacancies/Unit", y = "Bad Debt Expense/Unit", caption = paste("Excludes ", nrow(filter(joinedData, TOTAL_VACANCIES_byUnit > 1000))," HDC projects with Total Vacancies > ", 1000, sep = "")) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(legend.position="none", axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 15, b = 0, l=0))) 

#Display Graph
vac_bde

#lm()
#mux vac by -1 
#include dollar sign 
#perc of GROSS POT RENT 
#
#----Section: Task Outputs----

#9. Scatterplot of DSCR1 (note: this is NOI / annual mortgage payments for HDC mortgages only) vs. bad debt expense per unit. 

#Note: we like to see DSCR > 1.15 or 1.2, depending on how the denominator, debt service, is defined. DSCR < 1 means that a building likely can’t pay its full mortgage bill.


joinedData$DSCR <- abs(joinedData$DSCR1)
joinedData$DSCR1[joinedData$DSCR1 == 0] <- NA

dscr1_bde <- joinedData %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = DSCR1, y = bad_debt_ex_byUnit)) + 
  #fill = "dodgerblue3" 
  #aes(color= new_program_type))
  geom_point() +
  #scale_color_manual(values=c('light blue','dark Gray')) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200),lim = c(0, 1000), labels=scales::dollar_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 4, 1),lim = c(0,  4) ) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE,colour="black") + #linear regression for best fit line
  labs(title = "Debt Service Coverage Ratio 1 vs. Bad Debt Expense Per Unit", x = "DSCR1/Unit", y = "Bad Debt Expense/Unit", caption = paste("Excludes ", nrow(filter(joinedData, DSCR1 > 4))," HDC projects with DSCR1 > ", 4," and bde > $1000", sep = "")) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(legend.position="none",axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 15, b = 0, l=0))) 

#Display Graph
#dscr1_bde
#lm()
#HDC Mort, fin health


#Display Graph
dscr1_bde

#----Section: Task Outputs----
#HDC + More
#10. Scatterplot of DSCR2 (note: this is NOI / annual mortgage payments for all mortgages) vs. bad debt expense per unit.
#DSCR  NOI / annual mortgage payments for all mortgages. When underwriting a project, we expect a ** increase/decrease in bde per year. 

dscr2_bde <- joinedData %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = DSCR2, y = bad_debt_ex_byUnit)) + 
  #fill = "dodgerblue3"
  geom_point() +
  #scale_color_manual(values=c('light blue','dark Gray')) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200),lim = c(0, 1000), labels=scales::dollar_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 4, 1),lim = c(0,  4) ) +

  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE,colour="black") + #linear regression for best fit line
  labs(title = "Debt Service Coverage Ratio 2 vs. Bad Debt Expense Per Unit", x = "DSCR2/Unit", y = "Bad Debt Expense/Unit", caption = paste("Excludes ", nrow(filter(joinedData, DSCR2 > 4))," HDC projects with DSCR2 > ", 4, "and bde > $1000",sep = "")) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(legend.position="none",axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 15, b = 0, l=0))) 

#Display Graph
dscr2_bde

#lm()
#-4/4
